The most severe judgments, by themselves, will not humble or change the hearts of sinners; nothing, except the blood of Jesus Christ, can atone for the guilt of sin; nothing, except the sanctifying Spirit of God, can purge away its pollution. The priests and the Levites had gone to Judah and Jerusalem, but instead of them God raised up prophets who read and explained the word. They likely were from the schools of the prophets, first established by Samuel. They did not have the spirit of prophecy like Elijah, but taught the people to remain faithful to the God of Israel. These prophets Jezebel sought to destroy. The few that escaped death were forced to hide themselves. God has his remnant among all kinds of people, both important and unimportant; and that faith, fear, and love of his name, which are the results of the Holy Spirit, will be accepted through the Redeemer. See how wonderfully God raises up friends for his ministers and people, for their protection in difficult times. Food and water were now scarce, yet Obadiah would find enough for God’s prophets to keep them alive. Ahab was concerned about not losing all of his animals; but he did not care about his soul, about not losing that. He took pains to seek grass, but none to seek the favor of God; protecting against the effect, but not inquiring how to remove the cause. But it is a good sign for a people when God calls his ministers to stand forth and show themselves. And we may better endure hardship while our eyes see our teachers.

One can guess how people feel about God by observing how they feel about his people and ministers. It has been the fate of the best and most useful men, like Elijah, to be called and considered troublers of the land. But those who cause God’s judgments do the harm, not he who foretells them and warns the nation to repent.

Many of the people wavered in their judgment and varied in their practice. Elijah called upon them to decide whether Jehovah or Baal was the self-existent, supreme God, the Creator, Governor, and Judge of the world, and to follow him alone. It is dangerous to hesitate between the service of God and the service of sin, the rule of Christ and the rule of our desires. If Jesus is the only Savior, let us cling to him alone for everything; if the Bible is the word of God, let us respect and accept the whole of it, and submit our understanding to the Divine teaching it contains. Elijah proposed to bring the matter to a trial. Baal had all the outward advantages, but the event encourages all of God’s witnesses and advocates never to fear the face of man. The God that answers by fire, let him be God; the atonement was to be made by sacrifice before the judgment could be removed in mercy. The God therefore that has power to pardon sin and to signify it by consuming the sin-offering must needs be the God that can relieve from calamity. God never required his worshippers to honor him in the manner of the worshippers of Baal; but the service of the devil, though sometimes it pleases and pampers the body, really is cruel to it, as in envy and drunkenness. God requires that we mortify our desires and corruptions; but bodily penances and severities are no pleasure to him. Who has required these things at your hands? A few words uttered in assured faith and with fervent affection for the glory of God and love to the souls of men, or thirstings after the Lord’s image and his favor, form the effective, fervent prayer of the righteous man, which avails much. Elijah sought not his own glory, but that of God, for the good of the people. The people are all agreed, convinced, and satisfied; Jehovah, he is the God. Some, we hope, had their hearts turned, but most of them were convinced only, not converted. Blessed are they that have not seen what these saw, yet have believed, and have been moved by it more than those who saw it.

Israel, being so far reformed as to acknowledge the Lord to be God and to consent to the execution of Baal’s prophets, was so far accepted that God poured out blessing upon the land. Elijah long continued praying. Though the answer to our fervent and believing requests does not come quickly, we must continue earnest in prayer and not faint or give up. A little cloud at length appeared, which soon overspread the heavens and watered the earth. Great blessings often arise from small beginnings, showers of plenty from a cloud of span long. Let us never despise the day of small things but hope and wait for great things from it. From what small beginnings have great matters arisen! It is thus in all the gracious dealings of God with the soul. Scarcely to be perceived are the first workings of his Spirit in the heart, which grow up at last to the wonder of men and applause of angels. Elijah hastened Ahab home and attended him. God will strengthen his people for every service to which his commandments and providence call them. The awful displays of Divine justice and holiness dismay the sinner, extort confessions, and dispose to outward obedience while the impression lasts; but the view of these with mercy, love, and truth in Christ Jesus is needed to draw the soul to self-abasement, trust, and love. The Holy Spirit employs both in the conversion of sinners; when sinners are impressed with Divine truths, they should be exhorted to set about the duties to which the Savior calls his disciples.
